steps.title: Find the shared factor
steps.content: Both terms in $7x^2+4x$ contain a factor of $x$. The coefficients $7$ and $4$ do not share a factor greater than $1$, so the greatest common factor is just $x$.

steps.title: Divide each term
steps.content: After factoring out $x$, the first term leaves $7x$ and the second term leaves $4$. Those two pieces go inside one set of parentheses.

steps.title: Write the product
steps.content: The equivalent factored form is $$ 7x^2+4x=x(7x+4) $$ Distributing gives the original expression again. The answer is A.
